new_classes.definition_en: A spirituous beverage generally obtained by simple or successive distillations of rectified cereal spirits or ethyl alcohol with juniper berries and other aromatic substances (e.g. coriander, angelica root, anise, ginger). Only spirituous beverages that satisfy the definition of gin are classified here.

new_classes.label_de: Gin in Behältnissen mit einem Inhalt von ≤ 2 L (Unterposition 2208 50 11)
new_classes.definition_en: Gin as defined under heading 2208 50, presented in containers holding 2 litres or less.

new_classes.label_en: Gin distillation process (distillation of rectified cereal spirits or ethyl alcohol with juniper berries and other aromatic substances)
new_classes.label_de: Gin-Destillationsprozess (Destillation von rektifiziertem Getreidespirituosen oder Ethylalkohol mit Wacholderbeeren und anderen Aromastoffen)
new_classes.definition_en: A distillation process in which rectified cereal spirits or ethyl alcohol are distilled (simply or successively) together with juniper berries and optionally other aromatic substances such as coriander, angelica root, anise or ginger, resulting in gin.
Berries of the juniper plant (Juniperus communis) used as the characteristic botanical ingredient in the distillation or flavouring of gin.

new_classes.label_en: Rectified cereal spirit (base spirit for gin production)
new_classes.definition_en: A highly rectified (purified) spirit derived from fermented cereal grains, used as the base alcohol in the production of gin.

coverage_explanation: The principal distinguishing criteria from the legal text are captured: (1) gin is a spirituous beverage produced by distillation of rectified cereal spirits or ethyl alcohol with juniper berries and other aromatics (modelled via GinDistillationProcess requiring juniper berry as a part, linked to the Gin2208 class via producedBy); (2) the small-container variant ≤ 2 L (2208 50 11) is modelled as a subclass of Gin2208 that has part a SmallContainer. The coverage is not 1.0 because: (a) the legal note is truncated ("only spirituous beverages...") and the complete finishing criterion (minimum alcoholic strength, specific legal definition criteria) cannot be fully axiomatised from the truncated text; (b) the explicit enumeration of optional aromatics (coriander, angelica root, anise, ginger) is informative but not modelled as mandatory constraints, which is correct for optional ingredients; (c) no sibling class for large-container gin (2208 50 19) is in scope for this code node but the Gin2208 parent is shared.
